It stripes a large index across a fleet of small-RAM Meilisearch nodes with a configurable replication factor, fans out search queries across all shards, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all using the Meilisearch Community Edition.\n6\t\n7\t## The Problem\n8\t\n9\tMeilisearch loads its entire index into memory-mapped LMDB files. A large index that exceeds a single server's available RAM cannot run on that server. The Enterprise Edition's native sharding is gated behind a commercial license. Miroir solves this without it.\n10\t\n11\t## How It Works\n12\t\n13\t```\n14\tClient\n15\t  │\n16\t  ▼\n17\tMiroir Orchestrator\n18\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → assign to shard → write to R replicas\n19\t  ├── Read path:  scatter query to all shards → gather → merge ranked results\n20\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove → recompute assignments → migrate minimum shards\n21\t\n22\tMeilisearch Nodes (N instances, each holding a subset of shards)\n23\t  node-0   node-1   node-2   ...   node-N\n24\t```\n25\t\n26\t## Replication Factor\n27\t\n28\tAnalogous to software RAID — configurable per deployment:\n29\t\n30\t| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n31\t|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n32\t| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n33\t| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n34\t| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n35\t\n36\t## Key Components\n37\t\n38\t- **Orchestrator** — proxy that handles shard routing, scatter-gather, result merging, and topology management\n39\t- **Shard router** — consistent hash function (Rendezvous/HRW) mapping document IDs to node assignments; minimal reshuffling on topology change\n40\t- **Rebalancer** — on node add/remove, recomputes assignments and migrates only the shards that changed owners; surviving replicas serve reads during rebuild\n41\t- **Result merger** — normalizes and merges ranked result sets from multiple shards into a single coherent response\n42\t\n43\t## Status\n44\t\n45\tDesign phase. See [`docs/`](docs/) for architecture detail.\n46\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcea9875-42f3-40bf-9abf-7c9e9cc539b7","uuid":"515f1980-b33e-4455-8820-4dec281683aa","timestamp":"2026-05-09T16:04:11.809Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/README.md","content":"# Miroir\n\n**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n\nMiroir is a RAID-like orchestration layer for Meilisearch. It stripes a large index across a fleet of small-RAM Meilisearch nodes with a configurable replication factor, fans out search queries across all shards, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all using the Meilisearch Community Edition.\n\n## The Problem\n\nMeilisearch loads its entire index into memory-mapped LMDB files. A large index that exceeds a single server's available RAM cannot run on that server. The Enterprise Edition's native sharding is gated behind a commercial license. Miroir solves this without it.\n\n## How It Works\n\n```\nClient\n  │\n  ▼\nMiroir Orchestrator\n  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → assign to shard → write to R replicas\n  ├── Read path:  scatter query to all shards → gather → merge ranked results\n  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove → recompute assignments → migrate minimum shards\n\nMeilisearch Nodes (N instances, each holding a subset of shards)\n  node-0   node-1   node-2   ...   node-N\n```\n\n## Replication Factor\n\nAnalogous to software RAID — configurable per deployment:\n\n| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n\n## Key Components\n\n- **Orchestrator** — proxy that handles shard routing, scatter-gather, result merging, and topology management\n- **Shard router** — consistent hash function (Rendezvous/HRW) mapping document IDs to node assignments; minimal reshuffling on topology change\n- **Rebalancer** — on node add/remove, recomputes assignments and migrates only the shards that changed owners; surviving replicas serve reads during rebuild\n- **Result merger** — normalizes and merges ranked result sets from multiple shards into a single coherent response\n\n## Status\n\nDesign phase. See [`docs/`](docs/) for architecture detail.\n","numLines":46,"startLine":1,"totalLines":46}}}

Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n2\t\n3\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n4\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n5\tuse std::fmt;\n6\t\n7\t/// Unique identifier for a node.\n8\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n9\tpub struct NodeId(String);\n10\t\n11\timpl NodeId {\n12\t    /// Create a new NodeId.\n13\t    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n14\t        Self(id)\n15\t    }\n16\t\n17\t    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n18\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n19\t        &self.0\n20\t    }\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\timpl From<String> for NodeId {\n24\t    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n25\t        Self(s)\n26\t    }\n27\t}\n28\t\n29\timpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n30\t    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n31\t        &self.0\n32\t    }\n33\t}\n34\t\n35\t/// Health status of a node.\n36\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n37\tpub enum NodeStatus {\n38\t    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n39\t    Healthy,\n40\t    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n41\t    Degraded,\n42\t    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n43\t    Active,\n44\t    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n45\t    Joining,\n46\t    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n47\t    Draining,\n48\t    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n49\t    Failed,\n50\t    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n51\t    Removed,\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl NodeStatus {\n55\t    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n56\t    ///\n57\t    /// # State Transition Rules\n58\t    ///\n59\t    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n60\t    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n61\t    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n62\t    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n63\t    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n64\t    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n65\t    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n66\t    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n67\t    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n68\t    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n69\t    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n70\t        match (self, new_status) {\n71\t            // Initial state\n72\t            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n73\t\n74\t            // Normal operations\n75\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n76\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n77\t\n78\t            // Failure and recovery\n79\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n80\t            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n81\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n82\t\n83\t            // Degradation\n84\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n85\t            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n86\t            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n87\t\n88\t            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n89\t            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n90\t            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n91\t\n92\t            // Same state is always valid\n93\t            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n94\t\n95\t            // All other transitions are invalid\n96\t            _ => false,\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n101\t    ///\n102\t    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n103\t    ///\n104\t    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n105\t    ///\n106\t    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n107\t    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n108\t    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n109\t    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n110\t    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n111\t    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n112\t    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n113\t    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n114\t    ///\n115\t    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n116\t    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n117\t    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n118\t    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n119\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n120\t        match self {\n121\t            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n122\t            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n123\t            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n124\t        }\n125\t    }\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\timpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n129\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n130\t        match self {\n131\t            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n132\t            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n133\t            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n134\t            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n135\t            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n136\t            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n137\t            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n138\t        }\n139\t    }\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n143\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n144\tpub struct Node {\n145\t    /// Unique node identifier.\n146\t    pub id: NodeId,\n147\t\n148\t    /// Node base URL / address.\n149\t    pub address: String,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Current health status.\n152\t    pub status: NodeStatus,\n153\t\n154\t    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n155\t    pub replica_group: u32,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\timpl Node {\n159\t    /// Create a new node.\n160\t    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n161\t        Self {\n162\t            id,\n163\t            address,\n164\t            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n165\t            replica_group,\n166\t        }\n167\t    }\n168\t\n169\t    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n170\t    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n171\t        Self {\n172\t            id,\n173\t            address,\n174\t            status,\n175\t            replica_group,\n176\t        }\n177\t    }\n178\t\n179\t    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n180\t    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n181\t        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n185\t    ///\n186\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n187\t    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n188\t        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n189\t            self.status = new_status;\n190\t            Ok(())\n191\t        } else {\n192\t            Err(TransitionError {\n193\t                from: self.status,\n194\t                to: new_status,\n195\t            })\n196\t        }\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n200\t    ///\n201\t    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n202\t    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n203\t    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n204\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n205\t        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n206\t    }\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n210\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n211\tpub struct TransitionError {\n212\t    pub from: NodeStatus,\n213\t    pub to: NodeStatus,\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\timpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n217\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n218\t        write!(\n219\t            f,\n220\t            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n221\t            self.from, self.to\n222\t        )\n223\t    }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n227\t\n228\t/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n229\t///\n230\t/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n231\t/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n232\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n233\tpub struct Group {\n234\t    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n235\t    pub id: u32,\n236\t\n237\t    /// Nodes in this group.\n238\t    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\timpl Group {\n242\t    /// Create a new group.\n243\t    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n244\t        Self {\n245\t            id,\n246\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n247\t        }\n248\t    }\n249\t\n250\t    /// Add a node to this group.\n251\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n252\t        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n253\t            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n258\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n259\t        &self.nodes\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n263\t    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n264\t        self.nodes.len()\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n270\t    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n271\t        self.nodes\n272\t            .iter()\n273\t            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n274\t            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n275\t            .collect()\n276\t    }\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n280\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n281\tpub struct Topology {\n282\t    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n283\t    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n284\t\n285\t    /// Replica groups.\n286\t    groups: Vec<Group>,\n287\t\n288\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n289\t    rf: usize,\n290\t\n291\t    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n292\t    shards: u32,\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\timpl Topology {\n296\t    /// Create a new empty topology.\n297\t    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n298\t        Self {\n299\t            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n300\t            groups: Vec::new(),\n301\t            rf,\n302\t            shards,\n303\t        }\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    /// Add a node to the topology.\n307\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n308\t        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n309\t\n310\t        // Ensure group exists\n311\t        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n312\t            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n313\t        }\n314\t\n315\t        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n316\t        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Get a node by ID.\n320\t    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n321\t        self.nodes.get(id)\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n325\t    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n326\t        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    /// Get all nodes.\n330\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n331\t        self.nodes.values()\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    /// Get a group by ID.\n335\t    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n336\t        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Iterate over all groups.\n340\t    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n341\t        self.groups.iter()\n342\t    }\n343\t\n344\t    /// Get the replication factor.\n345\t    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n346\t        self.rf\n347\t    }\n348\t\n349\t    /// Get the number of shards.\n350\t    pub fn shards(&self) -> u32 {\n351\t        self.shards\n352\t    }\n353\t\n354\t    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n355\t    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n356\t        self.groups.len() as u32\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n360\t    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n361\t        self.group(group_id)\n362\t            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n363\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n364\t    }\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t#[cfg(test)]\n368\tmod tests {\n369\t    use super::*;\n370\t\n371\t    // --- Existing tests updated for address field ---\n372\t\n373\t    #[test]\n374\t    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n375\t        let mut node = Node::new(\n376\t            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n377\t            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n378\t            0,\n379\t        );\n380\t\n381\t        // Joining status is not healthy\n382\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n383\t\n384\t        // Healthy status is healthy\n385\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n386\t        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n387\t\n388\t        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n389\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n390\t        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n391\t\n392\t        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n393\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n394\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n395\t\n396\t        // Draining status is not healthy\n397\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n398\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n399\t\n400\t        // Failed status is not healthy\n401\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n402\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n403\t\n404\t        // Removed status is not healthy\n405\t        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n406\t        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n407\t    }\n408\t\n409\t    #[test]\n410\t    fn test_group_node_count() {\n411\t        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n412\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n413\t\n414\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n415\t        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n416\t\n417\t        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n418\t   fn test_node_set_status_invalid_transition() {\n610\t        let mut node = Node::with_status(\n611\t            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n612\t assert!(result.is_err());\n619\t        let err = result.unwrap_err();\n620\t        assert_eq!(err.from, NodeStatus::Removed);\n621\t        assert_eq!(err.to, NodeStatus::Active);\n622\t        // );\n717\t\n718\t        group.add_node(node1.id.clone());\n719\t        group.add_node(node2.id.clone());\n720\t        group.add_node(node3.id.clone());\n721\t\n722\t {\n773\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n774\t\n775\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n776\t            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n777\t            \"http://node1\".to_string(),\n778\t            0,\n779\t        ));\n780\t\n781\t        let node_id = NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string());\n782\t        {\n783\t            let node = assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n794\t    }\n795\t\n796\t    // --- Display tests ---\n797\t\n798\t    #[test]\n799\t    fn test_node_status_display() {\n800\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Healthy.to_string(), \"healthy\");\n801\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Degraded.to_string(), \"degraded\");\n802\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Active.to_string(), \"active\");\n803\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Joining.to_string(), \"joining\");\n804\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Draining.to_string(), \"draining\");\n805\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Failed.to_string(), \"failed\");\n806\t        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Removed.to_string(), \"removed\");\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    #[test]\n810\t    fn test_transition_error_display() {\n811\t        let err = TransitionError {\n812\t            from: NodeStatus::Joining,\n813\t            to: NodeStatus::Draining,\n814\t        };\n815\t        let msg = format!(\"{}\", err);\n816\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"invalid state transition\"));\n817\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"joining\"));\n818\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"draining\"));\n819\t    }\n820\t}\n821\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcea9875-42f3-40bf-9abf-7c9e9cc539b7","uuid":"b05d3a89-a88a-455f-ac35-23d47a95a097","timestamp":"2026-05-09T16:04:19.051Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/topology.rs","content":"//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard IS being actively migrated off this node\n    /// (use `None` if the shard is not being drained or no shard is being checked).\n    /// When `Some(...)`, the node is NOT eligible for writes.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => !draining_shard.is_some(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n    ///\n    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n        self.nodes\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n\n    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n    shards: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n            shards,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of shards.\n    pub fn shards(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.shards\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n\n    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n        self.group(group_id)\n            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // --- Existing tests updated for address field ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut    NodeStatus::Active,\n        ));\n\n        let healthy_group0 = topology.healthy_nodes_in_group(0);\n        assert_eq!(healthy_group0.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(healthy_group0[0].id.as_str(),   // --- Test for node mutation ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_node_mut() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://node1\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        let node_id = NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string());\n        {\n            let node = topology.node(&node_id).unwrap();\n            assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n        }\n\n        {\n            let node = topology.node_mut(&node_id).unwrap();\n       assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Active.to_string(), \"active\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Joining.to_string(), \"joining\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Draining.to_string(), \"draining\");\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_fcbd8c22d91d42e3b4970e6c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n12\t/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n13\t/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\n14\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n15\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n16\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n18\t    h.finish()\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n26\t/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\n27\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n28\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n29\t        .iter()\n30\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n31\t        .collect();\n32\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n33\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n34\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n35\t    });\n36\t    scored\n37\t        .into_iter()\n38\t        .take(rf)\n39\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n40\t        .collect()\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n44\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n45\t    topology\n46\t        .groups()\n47\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n48\t        .collect()\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n52\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n53\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n57\t///\n58\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n59\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n60\t/// map to the same node.\n61\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n62\t    (0..shard_count)\n63\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n64\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n65\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n66\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n67\t        })\n68\t        .collect()\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n72\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n73\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n74\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n75\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t#[cfg(test)]\n79\tmod tests {\n80\t    use super::*;\n81\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n82\t\n83\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n84\t    #[test]\n85\t    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n86\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n89\t            .collect();\n90\t        let shard_id = 42;\n91\t\n92\t        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n93\t        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n94\t\n95\t        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n99\t    #[test]\n100\t    fn test_score_stability() {\n101\t        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n102\t        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n103\t        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n104\t    }\n105\t\n106\t    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n107\t    #[test]\n108\t    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n109\t        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n110\t        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n111\t        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n112\t\n113\t        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n114\t        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n115\t        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n119\t    #[test]\n120\t    fn .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n128\t            .collect();\n129\t\n130\t        let shard_count = 100;\n131\t        let rf = 1;\n132\t\n133\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n134\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n136\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n137\t\n138\t            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n139\t            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n140\t                moved_count += 1;\n141\t            }\n142\t        }\n143\t\n144\t        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n145\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n146\t        assert!(\n147\t → each node holds 18–26 shards\n153\t    #[test]\n154\t    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n155\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n156\t            .into_iter()\n157\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n158\t            .collect();\n159\t        let shard_count = 64;\n160\t        let rf = 1;\n161\t\n162\t        let mut assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n192\t    #[test]\n193\t    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n194\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n195\t            .into_iter()\n196\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n197\t            .collect();\n198\t        let Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n342\t        let group_id = 0;\n343\t\n344\t        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n345\t        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n346\t            let node = );\n351\t            topology.add_node(node);\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n355\t        let shard_count = 10;\n356\t        let rf =     );\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n637\t    #[test]\n638\t    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n639\t score_correct, score_reversed,\n776\t            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n777\t        );\n778\t    }\n779\t}\n780\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcea9875-42f3-40bf-9abf-7c9e9cc539b7","uuid":"24fd3c42-e065-4ec2-9f07-2b8eba2045fe","timestamp":"2026-05-09T16:04:20.325Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n///\n/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n///\n/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n/// map to the same node.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n\n        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_stability() {\n        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n    }\n\n    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 100;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Debug: print actual distribution\n        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n        // This accommodates natural variance in hash-based distribution\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n    #[test]\n    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let rf = 2;\n        let shard_count = 100;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n\n        // Also verify that not everything changed\n        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count >= min_expected,\n            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n\n        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n        for group_id in 0..3 {\n            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n                let node = Node::new(\n                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n                    group_id,\n                );\n                topology.add_node(node);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n\n        // All targets should be unique\n        let unique: std::collections::HashSet<_> = targets.iter().collect();\n        assert_eq!(unique.len(), 6);\n\n        // Each replica group should contribute exactly RF nodes\n        for group in topology.groups() {\n            let group_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|t| group.nodes().contains(t))\n                .collect();\n            assert_eq!(\n                group_targets.len(),\n                topology.rf(),\n                \"Group {} should contribute exactly RF nodes\",\n                group.id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n        let queries = 1000u64;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n        for seq in 0..queries {\n            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n                count,\n                expected - 1,\n                expected + 1\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n        let num_nodes = 5;\n\n        // Add nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..num_nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n        let query_seq = 0;\n\n        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n\n        // All nodes should be from the group\n        for node in &covering {\n            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n    #[test]\n    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n\n        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n                group_id,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n        let shard_count = 10;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n\n        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n            if n0 != n1 {\n                rotated_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n        assert!(\n            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n        let key = \"user:12345\";\n        let shard_count = 64;\n\n        let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n        let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n\n        assert_eq!(shard1, shard2);\n        assert!(shard1 < shard_count);\n    }\n\n    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_distribution() {\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let keys = 1000;\n\n        let mut counts = vec![0; shard_count as usize];\n        for i in 0..keys {\n            let key = format!(\"user:{i}\");\n            let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n            counts[shard as usize] += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n        for count in counts {\n            // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n            assert!(\n                count >= expected / 2 && count <= expected * 2,\n                \"Shard count {count} outside reasonable range\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\", \"node5\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        for rf in 1..=5 {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            assert_eq!(\n                assignment.len(),\n                rf,\n                \"Assignment should return exactly RF nodes\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Test 14: assign_shard_in_group handles RF larger than node count\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_rf_larger_than_nodes() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let rf = 5;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all available nodes when RF > node count\n        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), nodes.len());\n    }\n\n    // Test 15: Empty node list returns empty assignment\n    #[test]\n    fn test_assign_shard_empty_nodes() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![];\n        let shard_id = 42;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        assert!(assignment.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    // Test 16: write_targets with empty topology\n    #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_empty_topology() {\n        let topology = Topology::new(64, 2);\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        assert!(targets.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    // Test 17: shard_for_key with zero shard_count handles edge case\n    #[test]\n    #[should_panic(expected = \"attempt to calculate the remainder with a divisor of zero\")]\n    fn test_shard_for_key_zero_shard_count() {\n        // This test verifies the panic behavior - in production this should be validated\n        // at the API boundary\n        shard_for_key(\"test\", 0);\n    }\n\n    // Test 18: Group-scoped assignment prevents same-group replica placement\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_scoped_assignment() {\n        // Create topology with 2 groups, 2 nodes each\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1); // 64 shards, RF=1\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        // Group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g0n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g0n0\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g0n1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g0n1\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        // Group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n0\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n1\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        // With RG=2, RF=1, should get 2 targets (one from each group)\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 2);\n\n        // Verify one from each group\n        let g0_target = targets.iter().any(|t| {\n            topology\n                .node(t)\n                .map(|n| n.replica_group == 0)\n                .unwrap_or(false)\n        });\n        let g1_target = targets.iter().any(|t| {\n            topology\n                .node(t)\n                .map(|n| n.replica_group == 1)\n                .unwrap_or(false)\n        });\n\n        assert!(g0_target, \"Should have one target from group 0\");\n        assert!(g1_target, \"Should have one target from group 1\");\n    }\n\n    // === Acceptance Tests (plan §8 \"Router correctness\") ===\n\n    // AT-1: Determinism: same (shard_id, nodes) → identical Vec<NodeId> across 1000 randomized runs\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_determinism_1000_runs() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        for run in 0..1000 {\n            let shard_id = (run % 100) as u32; // Test different shard IDs\n            let rf = ((run % 3) + 1) as usize; // Test different RF values\n\n            let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n            assert_eq!(\n                assignment1, assignment2,\n                \"Assignments differ on run {}: shard_id={}, rf={}\",\n                run, shard_id, rf\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-2: Reshuffle bound on add: 64 shards, 3→4 nodes → at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges differ\n        let max_expected = (2.0 * (1.0 / 4.0) * shard_count as f64).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n\n            // Count edges that differ\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n\n            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n            let diff = set_4.symmetric_difference(&set_3).count();\n            if diff > 0 {\n                moved_count += diff;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32 edges differ\n        // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n        let expected = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n        let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.9).ceil() as usize; // ±90%\n        assert!(\n            moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or removed\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_rf2_placement_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node should affect minimally\n        // Expected: roughly 1/4 of assignments might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-6: shard_for_key uses seed 0 and matches known fixture\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known fixture values computed with XxHash64::with_seed(0)\n        // These are verified against the actual twox-hash implementation\n        let fixtures = [\n            (\"user:12345\", 64, 15),\n            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n            (\"order:99999\", 64, 4),\n            (\"test\", 16, 10),\n            (\"hello\", 32, 6),\n        ];\n\n        for (key, shard_count, expected_shard) in fixtures {\n            let shard = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n            assert_eq!(\n                shard, expected_shard,\n                \"shard_for_key(\\\"{}\\\", {}) should be {}, got {}\",\n                key, shard_count, expected_shard, shard\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-7: Tie-breaking on node_id for identical scores\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_tie_breaking_node_id() {\n        // Create nodes that will have deterministic assignment\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node-a\", \"node-b\", \"node-c\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let rf = 3; // Request all nodes\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all nodes in a deterministic order\n        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), 3);\n\n        // The order should be stable across calls\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n        assert_eq!(assignment, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // AT-8: Canonical concatenation order (shard_id, node_id)\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n        let shard_id = 42u32;\n        let node_id = \"node1\";\n\n        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n\n        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n\n        // These should almost certainly be different\n        assert_ne!(\n            score_correct, score_reversed,\n            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":780,"startLine":1,"totalLines":780}}}
