Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n5\tuse serde_json::Value;\n6\tuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n7\t\n8\t/// Input to the merge function.\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n10\tpub struct MergeInput {\n11\t    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n12\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n13\t\n14\t    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n15\t    pub offset: usize,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n18\t    pub limit: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n21\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n24\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n29\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n30\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n31\t    pub body: Value,\n32\t\n33\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n34\t    pub success: bool,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\t/// Merged search result.\n38\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n39\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n40\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n41\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n42\t\n43\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n44\t    pub facets: Value,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n47\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n50\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n53\t    pub degraded: bool,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n57\t///\n58\t/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n59\t/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n60\t/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n61\t/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n62\t/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n63\t/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n64\t/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n65\t/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n66\t/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n67\t///\n68\t/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n69\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\n70\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n71\t    // Filter to only successful responses\n72\t    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n73\t        .shard_hits\n74\t        .iter()\n75\t        .filter(|s| s.success)\n76\t        .collect();\n77\t\n78\t    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n79\t    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n80\t\n81\t    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n82\t    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n83\t\n84\t    for shard in &successful_shards {\n85\t        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n86\t            for hit in hits {\n87\t                let score = hit\n88\t                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n89\t                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n90\t                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n91\t\n92\t                let primary_key = hit\n93\t                    .get(\"id\")\n94\t                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n95\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n96\t                    .to_string();\n97\t\n98\t                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n99\t                    score,\n100\t                    primary_key,\n101\t                    hit: hit.clone(),\n102\t                });\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n108\t    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n109\t    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n110\t        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n111\t        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n112\t        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n113\t        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n114\t        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n115\t\n116\t        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n117\t        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n118\t        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n119\t\n120\t        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n121\t            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n122\t                Some(self.cmp(other))\n123\t            }\n124\t        }\n125\t\n126\t        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n127\t            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n128\t                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n129\t                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n130\t                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n131\t                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n132\t                    }\n133\t                    Some(ord) => ord,\n134\t                }\n135\t            }\n136\t        }\n137\t\n138\t        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n139\t        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n140\t        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n141\t\n142\t        for hit in all_hits {\n143\t            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n144\t                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n145\t            } else {\n146\t                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n147\t                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n148\t                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n149\t                    // Compare using the score directly\n150\t                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n151\t                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n152\t                        heap.pop();\n153\t                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n154\t                    }\n155\t                }\n156\t            }\n157\t        }\n158\t\n159\t        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n160\t        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n161\t        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n162\t        result\n163\t    } else {\n164\t        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n165\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n166\t        all_hits\n167\t    };\n168\t\n169\t    // Apply offset and limit\n170\t    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n171\t        .into_iter()\n172\t        .skip(input.offset)\n173\t        .take(input.limit)\n174\t        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n175\t            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n176\t\n177\t            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n178\t            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n179\t                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n180\t            }\n181\t\n182\t            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n183\t            if !input.client_requested_score {\n184\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n185\t                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n186\t                }\n187\t            }\n188\t\n189\t            hit.clone()\n190\t        })\n191\t        .collect();\n192\t\n193\t    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n194\t    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n195\t\n196\t    // Sum estimated total hits\n197\t    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n198\t        .iter()\n199\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n200\t        .sum();\n201\t\n202\t    // Max processing time across all shards\n203\t    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n204\t        .iter()\n205\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n206\t        .max()\n207\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n208\t\n209\t    MergedSearchResult {\n210\t        hits: page,\n211\t        facets,\n212\t        estimated_total_hits,\n213\t        processing_time_ms,\n214\t        degraded,\n215\t    }\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n219\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n220\tstruct HitWithScore {\n221\t    score: f64,\n222\t    primary_key: String,\n223\t    hit: Value,\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n227\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n228\t        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n229\t    }\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\timpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n233\t\n234\timpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n235\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n236\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n237\t    }\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl HitWithScore {\n241\t    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n242\t    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n243\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n244\t        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n245\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n246\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n247\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n248\t            }\n249\t            Some(ord) => ord,\n250\t        }\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\timpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n255\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n256\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n257\t        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n258\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n259\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n260\t                // Reversed for descending order\n261\t                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n262\t            }\n263\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n269\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n270\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n273\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n274\t    /// and facet aggregation.\n275\t    fn merge(\n276\t        &self,\n277\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n278\t        offset: usize,\n279\t        limit: usize,\n280\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n281\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t/// Response from a single shard.\n285\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n286\tpub struct ShardResponse {\n287\t    /// Shard identifier.\n288\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n289\t\n290\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n291\t    pub body: Value,\n292\t\n293\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n294\t    pub success: bool,\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n298\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n299\tpub struct MergedResult {\n300\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n301\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n302\t\n303\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n304\t    pub facets: Value,\n305\t\n306\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n307\t    pub total_hits: u64,\n308\t\n309\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n310\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n311\t\n312\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n313\t    pub degraded: bool,\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n317\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n318\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n319\t\n320\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n321\t    fn merge(\n322\t        &self,\n323\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n324\t        offset: usize,\n325\t        limit: usize,\n326\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n327\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n328\t        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n329\t        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n330\t            .into_iter()\n331\t            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n332\t                body: sr.body,\n333\t                success: sr.success,\n334\t            })\n335\t            .collect();\n336\t\n337\t        // Call the new merge function\n338\t        let input = MergeInput {\n339\t            shard_hits,\n340\t            offset,\n341\t            limit,\n342\t            client_requested_score,\n343\t            facets: None,\n344\t        };\n345\t\n346\t        let result = merge(input);\n347\t\n348\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n349\t            hits: result.hits,\n350\t            facets: result.facets,\n351\t            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n352\t            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n353\t            degraded: result.degraded,\n354\t        })\n355\t    }\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n359\t///\n360\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n361\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\n364\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n365\t    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n366\t\n367\t    for shard in shards {\n368\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n369\t            .body\n370\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n371\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n372\t        {\n373\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n374\t                // Apply facet filter if provided\n375\t                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n376\t                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n377\t                        continue;\n378\t                    }\n379\t                }\n380\t\n381\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n382\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n383\t\n384\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n385\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n386\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                }\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n394\t    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n395\t        .into_iter()\n396\t        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n397\t            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n398\t                .into_iter()\n399\t                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n400\t                .collect();\n401\t            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n402\t        })\n403\t        .collect();\n404\t\n405\t    Value::Object(result)\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n409\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n410\tpub struct StubMerger;\n411\t\n412\timpl Merger for StubMerger {\n413\t    fn merge(\n414\t        &self,\n415\t        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n416\t        _offset: usize,\n417\t        _limit: usize,\n418\t        _client_requested_score: bool,\n419\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n420\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n421\t            hits: Vec::new(),\n422\t            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n423\t            total_hits: 0,\n424\t            processing_time_ms: 0,\n425\t            degraded: false,\n426\t        })\n427\t    }\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t#[cfg(test)]\n431\tmod tests {\n432\t    use super::*;\n433\t\n434\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n435\t        serde_json::json!({\n436\t            \"id\": id,\n437\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n438\t            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n439\t        })\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n443\t        ShardResponse {\n444\t            shard_id,\n445\t            body: serde_json::json!({\n446\t                \"hits\": hits,\n447\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n448\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n449\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n450\t            }),\n451\t            success: true,\n452\t        }\n453\t    }\n454\t\n455\t    #[test]\n456\t    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n457\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n458\t\n459\t        let hits1 = vec![\n460\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n461\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n462\t            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n463\t        ];\n464\t        let hits2 = vec![\n465\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n466\t            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n467\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n468\t        ];\n469\t\n470\t        let shards = vec![\n471\t            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n472\t            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n473\t        ];\n474\t\n475\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n476\t\n477\t        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n478\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n479\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n480\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n481\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n482\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n483\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n484\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    #[test]\n488\t    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n489\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n490\t\n491\t        let hits = vec![\n492\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n493\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n494\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n495\t            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n496\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n497\t        ];\n498\t\n499\t        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0,        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n506\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    #[test]\n510\t    fn   let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n516\t\n517\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n518\t\n519\t        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n520\t        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n521\t        assert!(result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    #[test]\n525\t    fn let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n531\t\n532\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n533\t\n534\t        // _rankingScore should be present\n535\t     \"facetDistribution\": {\n562\t                \"color\": {\n563\t                    \"red\": 10,\n564\t                    \"blue\": 5,\n565\t                },\n566\t                \"size\": 150 + 75\n627\t    }\n628\t\n629\t    #[test]\n630\t    fn test_processing_time_max_across_shards() {\n631\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n632\t\n633\t        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n634\t            \"hits\": [],\n635\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n636\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n637\t        });\n638\t\n639\t        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n640\t            \"hits\": [],\n641\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n642\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 25,\n643\t        });\n644\t\n645\t        let shard3 = serde_json::json!({\n646\t            \"hits\": [],\n647\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n648\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n649\t        });\n650\t\n651\t        let shards = vec![\n652\t            ShardResponse {\n653\t                shard_id: 0,\n654\t                body: shard1,\n655\t                success: true,\n656\t            },\n657\t            ShardResponse {\n658\t                shard_id: 1,\n659\t                body: shard2,\n660\t                success: true,\n661\t            },\n662\t            ShardResponse {\n663\t                shard_id: 2,\n664\t                body: shard3,\n665\t                success: true,\n666\t            },\n667\t        ];\n668\t\n669\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n670\t\n671\t        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 25); // max(10, 25, 15)\n672\t    }\n673\t\n674\t    #[test]\n675\t    fn test_degraded_flag_when_shard_fails() {\n676\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n677\t\n678\t        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n679\t\n680\t        let shards = vec![\n681\t            create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 1),\n682\t }\n1008\t}\n1009\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bcea9875-42f3-40bf-9abf-7c9e9cc539b7","uuid":"0a138640-5167-486e-bead-9e52de77dbf9","timestamp":"2026-05-09T16:04:36.389Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n\n/// Input to the merge function.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergeInput {\n    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n\n    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n    pub offset: usize,\n\n    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n    pub limit: usize,\n\n    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n\n    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardHitPage {\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergedSearchResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n///\n/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n///\n/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\npub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n    // Filter to only successful responses\n    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n        .shard_hits\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|s| s.success)\n        .collect();\n\n    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n\n    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n\n    for shard in &successful_shards {\n        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n            for hit in hits {\n                let score = hit\n                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n                let primary_key = hit\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                    .to_string();\n\n                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n                    score,\n                    primary_key,\n                    hit: hit.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n\n        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n\n        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n                Some(self.cmp(other))\n            }\n        }\n\n        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n                    }\n                    Some(ord) => ord,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n\n        for hit in all_hits {\n            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n            } else {\n                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n                    // Compare using the score directly\n                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n                        heap.pop();\n                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        result\n    } else {\n        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        all_hits\n    };\n\n    // Apply offset and limit\n    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n        .into_iter()\n        .skip(input.offset)\n        .take(input.limit)\n        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n\n            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n            }\n\n            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n            if !input.client_requested_score {\n                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            hit.clone()\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n\n    // Sum estimated total hits\n    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .sum();\n\n    // Max processing time across all shards\n    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    MergedSearchResult {\n        hits: page,\n        facets,\n        estimated_total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms,\n        degraded,\n    }\n}\n\n/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct HitWithScore {\n    score: f64,\n    primary_key: String,\n    hit: Value,\n}\n\nimpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n\nimpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n        Some(self.cmp(other))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl HitWithScore {\n    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n                // Reversed for descending order\n                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\npub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n    /// and facet aggregation.\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardResponse {\n    /// Shard identifier.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergedResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default implementation of Merger.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MergerImpl;\n\nimpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n                body: sr.body,\n                success: sr.success,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Call the new merge function\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits,\n            offset,\n            limit,\n            client_requested_score,\n            facets: None,\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input);\n\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: result.hits,\n            facets: result.facets,\n            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n            degraded: result.degraded,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n///\n/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n///\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\nfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    for shard in shards {\n        if let Some(facets) = shard\n            .body\n            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n        {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                // Apply facet filter if provided\n                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n\n                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n                .collect();\n            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Value::Object(result)\n}\n\n/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubMerger;\n\nimpl Merger for StubMerger {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        _offset: usize,\n        _limit: usize,\n        _client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n            total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            degraded: false,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n        ShardResponse {\n            shard_id,\n            body: serde_json::json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n            success: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits1 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n        ];\n        let hits2 = vec![\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![\n            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 5)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 2, 2, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Should skip first 2, take next 2\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_facet_counts_summed_across_shards() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shard1 = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n148\t\n149\t        match scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], get_request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n150\t            Ok(resp) => {\n151\t                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n152\t                    let original_value = if r.status == 200 {\n153\t                        Some(serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null))\n154\t                    } else {\n155\t                        None\n156\t                    };\n157\t                    rollback_values.insert(\n158\t                        target.as_str().to_string(),\n159\t                        RollbackValue {\n160\t                            original_value,\n161\t                            existed: r.status == 200,\n162\t                        },\n163\t                    );\n164\t                }\n165\t            }\n166\t            Err(_) => {\n167\t                // Node is already down, skip rollback for it\n168\t                rollback_values.insert(target.as_str().to_string(), RollbackValue {\n169\t                    original_value: None,\n170\t                    existed: false,\n171\t                });\n172\t            }\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n177\t\n178\t    let request = ScatterRequest {\n179\t        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n180\t        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n181\t        body: body_bytes,\n182\t        headers: vec![],\n183\t    };\n184\t\n185\t    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n186\t\n187\t    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n188\t    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n189\t    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n190\t\n191\t    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n192\t    for target in &targets {\n193\t        let result = scatter\n194\t            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n195\t            .await;\n196\t\n197\t        match result {\n198\t            Ok(resp) => {\n199\t                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n200\t                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n201\t                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n202\t                        let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n203\t                        last_response = Some(body);\n204\t                    } else {\n205\t                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n206\t                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n207\t                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n208\t                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n209\t                            target.as_str(),\n210\t                            r.status\n211\t                        )));\n212\t                    }\n213\t                }\n214\t            }\n215\t            Err(e) => {\n216\t                // Rollback from successful nodes\n217\t                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n218\t                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n219\t                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n220\t                    target.as_str(),\n221\t                    e\n222\t                )));\n223\t            }\n224\t        }\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n228\t        body\n229\t    } else {\n230\t        let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n231\t        serde_json::json!({\n232\t            \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n233\t            \"indexUid\": index,\n234\t            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n235\t            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n236\t            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n237\t        })\n238\t    };\n239\t\n240\t    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\n244\tasync fn rollback_setting(\n245\t    state: &ProxyState,\n246\t    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n247\t    successful_nodes: &[String],\n248\t    rollback_values: &std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue>,\n249\t    index: &str,\n250\t    setting_path: &str,\n251\t) {\n252\t    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n253\t        if let Some(rollback) = rollback_values.get(node_id) {\n254\t            let node_id_ref = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n255\t            if rollback.existed {\n256\t                // Restore original value\n257\t                if let Some(original) = &rollback.original_value {\n258\t                    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(original).unwrap_or_default();\n259\t                    let _ = state\n260\t                        .client\n261\t                        .send_to_node(\n262\t                            topology,\n263\t                            &node_id_ref,\n264\t                            \"PUT\",\n265\t                            &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n266\t                            Some(&body_bytes),\n267\t                            &[],\n268\t                        )\n269\t                        .await;\n270\t                }\n271\t            } else {\n272\t                // Setting didn't exist before, delete it\n273\t                let _ = state\n274\t                    .client\n275\t                    .send_to_node(\n276\t                        topology,\n277\t                        &node_id_ref,\n278\t                        \"DELETE\",\n279\t                        &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n280\t                        None,\n281\t                        &[],\n282\t                    )\n283\t                    .await;\n284\t            }\n285\t        }\n286\t    }\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\n290\tasync fn get_filterable_attributes(\n291\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n292\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n293\t) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n294\t    get_setting(&state, &index, \"filterable-attributes\").await\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\n298\tasync fn update_filterable_attributes(\n299\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n300\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n301\t    body: Value,\n302\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n303\t    // Ensure _miroir_shard is always in filterable attributes\n304\t    let mut updated = body.clone();\n305\t    if let Some(arr) = updated.as_array_mut() {\n306\t        if !arr.iter().any(|v| v.as_str() == Some(\"_miroir_shard\")) {\n307\t            arr.push(serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\"));\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &updated).await\n311\t}\n312\t\n313\t/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\n314\tasync Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index))\n608\t}\n609\t\n610\t/// Generic DELETE handler for resetting a setting to default.\n611\tasync fn delete_setting(\n612\t    state: &ProxyState,\n613\t }\n640\t\n641\t    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(serde_json::json!({}))).into_response())\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t#[cfg(test)]\n645\tmod tests {\n646\t\n647\t    #[test]\n648\t    fn Settings routes: GET, PATCH, DELETE /indexes/:index/settings\n//!\n//! Implements settings broadcast per plan §3:\n//! - Sequential apply-with-rollback on failure\n//! - Broadcast to all nodes\n//! - Rollback from successful nodes if any fail\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::write_targets,\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Settings router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(get_all_settings))\n        .route(\n            \"/filterable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_filterable_attributes).put(update_filterable_attributes).delete(delete_filterable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/searchable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_searchable_attributes).put(update_searchable_attributes).delete(delete_searchable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/sortable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_sortable_attributes).put(update_sortable_attributes).delete(delete_sortable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/displayed-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_displayed_attributes).put(update_displayed_attributes).delete(delete_displayed_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/ranking-rules\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_ranking_rules).put(update_ranking_rules).delete(delete_ranking_rules),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/stop-words\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_stop_words).put(update_stop_words).delete(delete_stop_words),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/synonyms\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_synonyms).put(update_synonyms).delete(delete_synonyms),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/distinct-attribute\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_distinct_attribute).put(update_distinct_attribute).delete(delete_distinct_attribute),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/typo-tolerance\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_typo_tolerance).put(update_typo_tolerance).delete(delete_typo_tolerance),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/faceting\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_faceting).put(update_faceting).delete(delete_faceting),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/pagination\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_pagination).put(update_pagination).delete(delete_pagination),\n        )\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings - Get all settings.\nasync fn get_all_settings(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query first available node\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n                        .unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::Null);\n                    return Ok(Json(body));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// Cached original value for rollback.\nstruct RollbackValue {\n    /// The original value to restore on rollback.\n    original_value: Option<Value>,\n    /// Whether the setting existed before.\n    existed: bool,\n}\n\n/// Generic handler for updating a setting with rollback.\nasync fn update_setting_with_rollback(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n    value: &Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    // Step 1: Fetch current values from all nodes for rollback\n    let mut rollback_values: std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for target in &targets {\n        let get_request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n            body: vec![],\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n        match scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], get_request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    let original_value = if r.status == 200 {\n                        Some(serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null))\n                    } else {\n                        None\n                    };\n                    rollback_values.insert(\n                        target.as_str().to_string(),\n                        RollbackValue {\n                            original_value,\n                            existed: r.status == 200,\n                        },\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Node is already down, skip rollback for it\n                rollback_values.insert(target.as_str().to_string(), RollbackValue {\n                    original_value: None,\n                    existed: false,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n    for target in &targets {\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n            .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n                        let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                        last_response = Some(body);\n                    } else {\n                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n                            target.as_str(),\n                            r.status\n                        )));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Rollback from successful nodes\n                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n                    target.as_str(),\n                    e\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n        body\n    } else {\n        let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n            \"indexUid\": index,\n            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n        })\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\nasync fn rollback_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n    successful_nodes: &[String],\n    rollback_values: &std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue>,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) {\n    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n        if let Some(rollback) = rollback_values.get(node_id) {\n            let node_id_ref = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n            if rollback.existed {\n                // Restore original value\n                if let Some(original) = &rollback.original_value {\n                    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(original).unwrap_or_default();\n                    let _ = state\n                        .client\n                        .send_to_node(\n                            topology,\n                            &node_id_ref,\n                            \"PUT\",\n                            &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                            Some(&body_bytes),\n                            &[],\n                        )\n                        .await;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // Setting didn't exist before, delete it\n                let _ = state\n                    .client\n                    .send_to_node(\n                        topology,\n                        &node_id_ref,\n                        \"DELETE\",\n                        &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                        None,\n                        &[],\n                    )\n                    .await;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn get_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"filterable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn update_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Ensure _miroir_shard is always in filterable attributes\n    let mut updated = body.clone();\n    if let Some(arr) = updated.as_array_mut() {\n        if !arr.iter().any(|v| v.as_str() == Some(\"_miroir_shard\")) {\n            arr.push(serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\"));\n        }\n    }\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &updated).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn delete_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Reset to default but always include _miroir_shard\n    let default = serde_json::json!([\"_miroir_shard\"]);\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &default).await\n}\n\n/// GET Tracing/logging + Prometheus middleware\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse prometheus::{Counter, Histogram, IntGauge, Registry, TextEncoder, HistogramOpts};\n\n/// Prometheus metrics registry.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Metrics {\n    pub registry: Registry,\n    requests_total: Counter,\n    request_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n    requests_in_flight: IntGauge,\n    degraded_requests_total: Counter,\n    no_quorum_requests_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // Create and register metrics\n        let requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let request_duration_seconds = Histogram::with_opts(HistogramOpts {\n            common_name: \"miroir_request_duration_seconds\".to_string(),\n            help: \"Request duration in seconds\".to_string(),\n            buckets: vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0],\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        let requests_in_flight = IntGauge::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_in_flight\",\n            \"Current number of requests in flight\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let degraded_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_degraded_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of degraded requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let no_quorum_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_no_quorum_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests that failed with no quorum\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(request_duration_seconds.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_in_flight.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(degraded_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(no_quorum_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            requests_total,\n            request_duration_seconds,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            degraded_requests_total,\n            no_quorum_requests_total,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request with labels.\n    pub fn record_request(&self, method: &str, path: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.requests_total.inc();\n        self.request_duration_seconds.observe(duration_secs);\n\n        // Check for no quorum (503 status)\n        if status == 503 {\n            self.no_quorum_requests_total.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a degraded request.\n    pub fn record_degraded(&self, method: &str, path: &str) {\n        self.degraded_requests_total.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment requests in flight.\n    pub fn inc_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement requests in flight.\n    pub fn dec_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.dec();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tracing middleware that logs each request.\npub async fn tracing_middleware(req: Request, next: Next) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let uri = req.uri().clone();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n    let status = response.status();\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        method = %method,\n        uri = %uri,\n        status = status.as_u16(),\n        duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),\n        \"request completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Prometheus metrics middleware.\npub async fn prometheus_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().to_string();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    state.metrics.inc_in_flight();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n    let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n    // Record metrics\n    state.metrics.record_request(&method, &path, status, duration);\n    state.metrics.dec_in_flight();\n\n    // Check for degraded header\n    if response.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\").is_some() {\n        state.metrics.record_degraded(&method, &path);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Export metrics in Prometheus text format.\npub fn export_metrics(metrics: &Metrics) -> String {\n    let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n    let metric_families = metrics.registry.gather();\n    let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n\n    encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer).unwrap();\n\n    String::from_utf8(buffer).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"# Failed to encode metrics\\n\".to_string())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_export_metrics() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        let output = export_metrics(&metrics);\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_total\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_default() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::default();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":191,"startLine":1,"totalLines":191}}}

Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5\n//!\n//! Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::State,\n    http::HeaderMap,\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n\n/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n    Master,\n    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n    Admin,\n}\n\n/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum AuthResult {\n    /// Valid token with its kind.\n    Valid(TokenKind),\n    /// No bearer token present.\n    None,\n    /// Invalid bearer token.\n    Invalid,\n}\n\n/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\npub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n        Some(h) => h,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n        Ok(s) => s,\n        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n    };\n\n    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n    }\n\n    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n    }\n\n    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n    AuthResult::Invalid\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires authentication.\npub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    true\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires admin access.\npub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n/// Authentication middleware.\n///\n/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let path = req.uri().path();\n\n    // Check if authentication is required\n    if !requires_auth(path) {\n        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n    }\n\n    // Validate bearer token\n    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n\n    match auth_result {\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n        }\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n            if requires_admin(path) {\n                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n            } else {\n                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n                ))\n            }\n        }\n        AuthResult::None => {\n            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n            ))\n        }\n        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Invalid API key\",\n                \"invalid_api_key\",\n            ))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn test_state() -> ProxyState {\n        let mut Some(t) = token {\n            headers.insert(\"authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {t}\").parse().unwrap());\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_master_key() {\n AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_admin_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"test-admin-key\"));\n\n Error-format parity test\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches expected shape\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\nstruct TestNode {\n    id: String,\n    base_url: String,\n}\n\nimpl TestNode {\n    fn new(id: impl Into<String>, port: u16) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: id.into(),\n            base_url: format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", port),\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn get(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn post(&self, path: &str, body: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .delete(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\nstruct TestCluster {\n    proxy_url: String,\n    nodes: Vec<TestNode>,\n}\n\nimpl TestCluster {\n    fn new(proxy_port: u16, node_ports: Vec<u16>) -> Self {\n        let nodes = node_ports\n            .into_iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, port)| TestNode::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i), port))\n            .collect();\n\n        Self {\n            proxy_url: format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", proxy_port),\n            nodes,\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn create_index(&self, uid: &str, primary_key: Option<&str>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        let mut body = serde_json::json!({ \"uid\": uid });\n        if let Some(pk) = primary_key {\n            body[\"primaryKey\"] = serde_json::json!(pk);\n        }\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes\", self.proxy_url))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn add_documents(&self, index: &str, documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .json(&documents)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn search(&self, index: &str, query: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn get_document(&self, index: &str, id: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\n                \"{}/indexes/{}/documents/{}\",\n                self.proxy_url, index, id\n            ))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn       .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn get_stats(&self, index: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore] // Requires running nodes\nasync fn test_1000_documents_indexed_retrievable_by_id() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"test_index\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    // Wait for index creation\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create 1000 documents\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..1000)\n        .map(|i| {\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"doc-{:05}\", i),\n                \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Add documents in batches\n    for chunk in documents.chunks(100) {\n        let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"test_index\", chunk.to_vec()).await;\n        assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n    }\n\n    // Wait for indexing\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Verify each document is retrievable by ID\n    for i in 0..1000 {\n        let id = format!(\"doc-{:05}\", i);\n        let resp = cluster.get_document(\"test_index\", &id).await;\n\n        assert!(\n            resp.status().is_success(),\n            \"Failed to retrieve document {}: status {}\",\n            id,\n            resp.status()\n        );\n\n        let doc: serde_json::Value = resp.json().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(doc[\"id\"], id);\n        assert_eq!(doc[\"value\"], i);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test: Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn cluster.create_index(\"search_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with unique keywords\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"unique-doc-{}\", i),\n                \"keyword\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"search_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n\n    // Search for each unique keyword and verify exactly one result\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        let keyword = format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i);\n        let search_resp = cluster\n            .search(\n                \"search_test\",\n                serde_json::json!({ \"q\": keyword, \"limit\": 100 }),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n        assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n        let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n        let hits = results[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            hits.len(),\n            1,\n            \"Expected exactly 1 result for keyword {}, got {}\",\n            keyword,\n            hits.len()\n        );\n\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"keyword\"], keyword);\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"value\"], i);\n    }\n\n    // Search without query should return all docs\n    let all_resp = cluster\n        .search(\"search_test\", serde_json::json!({ \"q\": \"\", \"limit\": 200 }))\n        .await;\n\n    let all_results: serde_json::Value = all_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n    let all_hits = all_results[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap();\n\n    // Check that we have 100 unique documents\n    let mut seen_ids = HashSet::new();\n    for hit in all_hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            seen_ids.insert(id),\n            \"Duplicate document ID found: {}\",\n            id\n        );\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(seen_ids.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 unique documents\");\n}\n\n/// Test: Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index with filterable attributes\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"facet_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/facet_test/settings/filterable-attributes\", cluster.proxy_url))\n        .json(&serde_json::json!([\"id\", \"color\", \"_miroir_shard\"]))\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(filter_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with 3 color values distributed across shards\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..300)\n        .map(|i| {\n            let color = match i % 3 {\n                0 => \"red\",\n                1 => \"blue\",\n                _ => \"green\",\n            };\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"color-doc-{}\", i),\n                \"color\": color,\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"facet_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Search with facets on color\n    let search_resp = cluster\n        .search(\n            \"facet_test\",\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"q\": \"\",\n                \"facets\": [\"color\"],\n                \"limit\": 0\n            }),\n        )\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n    let facet_dist = results[\"facetDistribution\"][\"color\"].as_object().unwrap();\n\n    // Verify each color has exactly 100 documents\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"red\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 red documents\"\n    );\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"blue\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 blue documents\"\n    );\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"green\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 green documents\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Test: Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_offset_limit_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"paging_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with sequential values\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n
