.collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"paging_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n\n    // Fetch all documents in pages\n    let mut all_values: Vec<i64> = Vec::new();\n    let page_size = 10;\n\n    for page in 0..10 {\n        let offset = page * page_size;\n        let search_resp = cluster\n            .search(\n                \"paging_test\",\n                serde_json::json!({\n                    \"q\": \"same text\",\n                    \"limit\": page_size,\n                    \"offset\": offset\n                }),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n      let value = hit[\"value\"].as_i64().unwrap();\n            all_values.push(value);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify we got exactly 100 unique values\n    assert_eq!(all_values.len(), 100);\n\n    // Verify global ordering is preserved (no duplicates, all 0-99 present)\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    for value in all_values {\n        assert!(\n            seen.insert(value),\n HTTP client for forwarding requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n//!\n//! Implements connection pooling, retries, and orchestrator-side\n//! retry cache for idempotency (plan §4 note on scatter.retry_on_timeout).\n\nuse miroir_core::config::ServerConfig;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Node response with status code and body (as Vec<u8> for scatter compatibility).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeResponse {\n    pub node_id: NodeId,\n    pub status: u16,\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,\n}\n\nimpl NodeResponse {\n    /// Convert body to JSON value.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn body_json(&self) -> Result<Value> {\n        serde_json::from_slice(&self.body).map_err(|e| MiroirError::Json(e))\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP client for scatter-gather requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct NodeClient {\n    /// Reqwest client with connection pooling.\n    client: Client,\n\n    /// Node master key for authentication.\n    node_master_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency.\n    /// Key: sha256(request_body || target_node || idempotency_key)\n    /// Value: cached terminal response\n    retry_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n}\n\n/// Cached response for retry deduplication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct CachedResponse {\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    response: NodeResponse,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient {\n    /// Create a new node client with connection pooling.\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, server_config: &ServerConfig) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .pool_max_idle_per_host(32)\n            .pool_idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))\n            .timeout(Duration::from_millis(server_config.request_timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_master_key: Arc::new(node_master_key),\n            retry_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node.\n    pub async fn send_to_node(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n        let node = topology\n            .node(node_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.address, path);\n\n        let mut request = match method {\n            \"GET\" => self.client.get(&url),\n            \"POST\" => self.client.post(&url),\n            \"PUT\" => self.client.put(&url),\n            \"PATCH\" => self.client.patch(&url),\n            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n            _ => {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n                )))\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Add node master key header\n        request = request.header(\n            \"Authorization\",\n            format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key.as_str()),\n        );\n\n        // Add custom headers\n        for (key, value) in headers {\n            request = request.header(key, value);\n        }\n\n        // Add body if present\n        if let Some(body_bytes) = body {\n            request = request.header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            request = request.body(body_bytes.to_vec());\n        }\n\n        let response = request.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"request to node {} failed: {e}\", node_id.as_str(),))\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n        // Collect response headers\n        let response_headers: Vec<(String, String)> = response\n            .headers()\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|(name, value)| {\n                value\n                    .to_str()\n                    .ok()\n                    .map(|v| (name.as_str().to_string(), v.to_string()))\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let body_bytes = response\n            .bytes()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"failed to read response body: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(NodeResponse {\n            node_id: node_id.clone(),\n            status,\n            body: body_bytes.to_vec(),\n            headers: response_headers,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Send requests to multiple nodes in parallel.\n    pub async fn send_to_many(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_ids: &[NodeId],\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Vec<Result<NodeResponse>> {\n        let futures: Vec<_> = node_ids\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| self.send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, body, headers))\n            .collect();\n\n        futures::future::join_all(futures).await\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old entries from the retry cache.\n    pub async fn prune_retry_cache(&self, max_age: Duration) {\n        let mut cache = self.retry_cache.write().await;\n        let now = std::time::Instant::now();\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < max_age);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":176,"startLine":1,"totalLines":176}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7513fdbd194c42cebe9378d8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Key: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n4\t//!\n5\t//! This cache prevents duplicate writes when retrying timed-out requests\n6\t//! to nodes. It stores terminal responses (success or definitive failure)\n7\t//! for a configurable TTL.\n8\t\n9\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n10\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n11\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n12\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n13\tuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n14\t\n15\t/// Cached response from a node.\n16\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n17\tpub struct CachedResponse {\n18\t    /// Response body.\n19\t    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n20\t    /// HTTP status code.\n21\t    pub status: u16,\n22\t    /// When this cache entry was created.\n23\t    pub cached_at: Instant,\n24\t}\n25\t\n26\timpl CachedResponse {\n27\t    /// Check if this entry is still valid (not expired).\n28\t    pub fn is_valid(&self, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n29\t        self.cached_at.elapsed() < ttl\n30\t    }\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Retry cache for idempotency.\n34\t#[derive(Clone)]\n35\tpub struct RetryCache {\n36\t    entries: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n37\t    default_ttl: Duration,\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\timpl RetryCache {\n41\t    /// Create a new retry cache with the specified default TTL.\n42\t    pub fn new(default_ttl: Duration) -> Self {\n43\t        Self {\n44\t            entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n45\t            default_ttl,\n46\t        }\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Generate cache key from request components.\n50\t    ///\n51\t    /// Key format: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n52\t    pub fn cache_key(\n53\t        batch: &[u8],\n54\t        target_node: &str,\n55\t        idempotency_key: Option<&str>,\n56\t    ) -> String {\n57\t        let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n58\t\n59\t        // Include batch\n60\t        hasher.update(batch);\n61\t\n62\t        // Include target node\n63\t        hasher.update(target_node.as_bytes());\n64\t\n65\t        // Include idempotency key or use empty string\n66\t        if let Some(key) = idempotency_key {\n67\t            hasher.update(key.as_bytes());\n68\t        }\n69\t\n70\t        let result = hasher.finalize();\n71\t        hex::encode(result)\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Get a cached response if it exists and is still valid.\n75\t    pub async fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<CachedResponse> {\n76\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n77\t        cache.get(key).and_then(|entry| {\n78\t            if entry.is_valid(self.default_ttl) {\n79\t                Some(entry.clone())\n80\t            } else {\n81\t                None\n82\t            }\n83\t        })\n84\t    }\n85\t\n86\t    /// Store a response in the cache.\n87\t    pub async fn put(&self, key: String, response: CachedResponse) {\n88\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n89\t        cache.insert(key, response);\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    /// Remove expired entries from the cache.\n93\t    pub async fn prune(&self) {\n94\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n95\t        let now = Instant::now();\n96\t\n97\t        cache.retain(|_, entry| {\n98\t            now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < self.default_ttl\n99\t        });\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t    /// Clear all entries from the cache.\n103\t    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n104\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n105\t        cache.clear();\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    /// Get the number of entries in the cache.\n109\t    pub async fn len(&self) -> usize {\n110\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n111\t        cache.len()\n112\t    }\n113\t\n114\t    /// Check if the cache is empty.\n115\t    pub async fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n116\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n117\t        cache.is_empty()\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t#[cfg(test)]\n122\tmod tests {\n123\t    use super::*;\n124\t\n125\t    #[test]\n126\t    fn test_cache_key_generation() {\n127\t        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n128\t        let target_node = \"node1\";\n129\t        let idempotency_key = Some(\"key123\");\n130\t\n131\t        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n132\t        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n133\t\n134\t        // Same inputs should produce same key\n135\t        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n136\t\n137\t        // Different inputs should produce different keys\n138\t        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, \"node2\", idempotency_key);\n139\t        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    #[test]\n143\t    fn test_cache_key_without_idempotency() Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n//!\n//! Key: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n//!\n//! This cache prevents duplicate writes when retrying timed-out requests\n//! to nodes. It stores terminal responses (success or definitive failure)\n//! for a configurable TTL.\n\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Cached response from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct CachedResponse {\n    /// Response body.\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n    /// HTTP status code.\n    pub status: u16,\n    /// When this cache entry was created.\n    pub cached_at: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl CachedResponse {\n    /// Check if this entry is still valid (not expired).\n    pub fn is_valid(&self, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n        self.cached_at.elapsed() < ttl\n    }\n}\n\n/// Retry cache for idempotency.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RetryCache {\n    entries: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n    default_ttl: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl RetryCache {\n    /// Create a new retry cache with the specified default TTL.\n    pub fn new(default_ttl: Duration) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            default_ttl,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Generate cache key from request components.\n    ///\n    /// Key format: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n    pub fn cache_key(\n        batch: &[u8],\n        target_node: &str,\n        idempotency_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> String {\n        let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n\n        // Include batch\n        hasher.update(batch);\n\n        // Include target node\n        hasher.update(target_node.as_bytes());\n\n        // Include idempotency key or use empty string\n        if let Some(key) = idempotency_key {\n            hasher.update(key.as_bytes());\n        }\n\n        let result = hasher.finalize();\n        hex::encode(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a cached response if it exists and is still valid.\n    pub async fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<CachedResponse> {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.get(key).and_then(|entry| {\n            if entry.is_valid(self.default_ttl) {\n                Some(entry.clone())\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Store a response in the cache.\n    pub async fn put(&self, key: String, response: CachedResponse) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.insert(key, response);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove expired entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn prune(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| {\n            now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < self.default_ttl\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of entries in the cache.\n    pub async fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the cache is empty.\n    pub async fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_generation() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n        let idempotency_key = Some(\"key123\");\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // Different inputs should produce different keys\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, \"node2\", idempotency_key);\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_without_idempotency() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // With and without idempotency key should be different\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, Some(\"key123\"));\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_put_get() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response.clone()).await;\n\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        let retrieved = retrieved.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.body, b\"test_response\");\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, 200);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_expiration() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response).await;\n\n        // Should be valid immediately\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n\n        // Wait for expiration\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n        // Should be expired now\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_prune() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response1 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response1\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let response2 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response2\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response1).await;\n        cache.put(\"key2\".to_string(), response2).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 2);\n\n        // Wait for expiration\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n        cache.prune().await;\n\n        // Should be empty after pruning\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_clear() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response.clone()).await;\n        cache.put(\"key2\".to_string(), response).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 2);\n\n        cache.clear().await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cached_response_is_valid() {\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: vec![],\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        assert!(response.is_valid(Duration::from_secs(60)));\n        assert!(!response.is_valid(Duration::from_millis(10)));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_is_empty() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        assert!(cache.is_empty().await);\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: vec![],\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response).await;\n\n        assert!(!cache.is_empty().await);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":282,"startLine":1,"totalLines":282}}}

Settings routes: GET, PATCH, DELETE /indexes/:index/settings\n//!\n//! Implements settings broadcast per plan §3:\n//! - Sequential apply-with-rollback on failure\n//! - Broadcast to all nodes\n//! - Rollback from successful nodes if any fail\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::write_targets,\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Settings router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(get_all_settings))\n        .route(\n            \"/filterable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_filterable_attributes).put(update_filterable_attributes).delete(delete_filterable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/searchable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_searchable_attributes).put(update_searchable_attributes).delete(delete_searchable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/sortable-attributes\",\n axum::routing::get(get_displayed_attributes).put(update_displayed_attributes).delete(delete_displayed_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/ranking-rules\",\n axum::routing::get(get_synonyms).put(update_synonyms).delete(delete_synonyms),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/distinct-attribute\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_distinct_attribute).put(update_distinct_attribute).delete(delete_distinct_attribute),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/typo-tolerance\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_typo_tolerance).put(update_typo_tolerance).delete(delete_typo_tolerance),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/faceting\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_faceting).put(update_faceting).delete(delete_faceting),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/pagination\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_pagination).put(update_pagination).delete(delete_pagination),\n        )\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings - Get all settings.\nasync fn get_all_settings(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query first available node\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n                        .unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::Null);\n                    return Ok(Json(body));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// Cached original value for rollback.\nstruct RollbackValue {\n    /// The original value to restore on rollback.\n    original_value: Option<Value>,\n    /// Whether the setting existed before.\n    existed: bool,\n}\n\n/// Generic handler for updating a setting with rollback.\nasync fn update_setting_with_rollback(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n    value: &Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    // Step 1: Fetch current values from all nodes for rollback\n    let mut rollback_values: std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for target in &targets {\n        let get_request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n            body: vec![],\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n        match scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], get_request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    let original_value = if r.status == 200 {\n                        Some(serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null))\n                    } else {\n                        None\n                    };\n                    rollback_values.insert(\n                        target.as_str().to_string(),\n                        RollbackValue {\n                            original_value,\n                            existed: r.status == 200,\n                        },\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Node is already down, skip rollback for it\n                rollback_values.insert(target.as_str().to_string(), RollbackValue {\n                    original_value: None,\n                    existed: false,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n    for target in &targets {\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n            .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n                        let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                        last_response = Some(body);\n                    } else {\n                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n                            target.as_str(),\n                            r.status\n                        )));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Rollback from successful nodes\n                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n                    target.as_str(),\n                    e\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n        body\n    } else {\n        let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n            \"indexUid\": index,\n            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n        })\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\nasync fn rollback_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n    successful_nodes: &[String],\n    rollback_values: &std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue>,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) {\n    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n        if let Some(rollback) = rollback_values.get(node_id) {\n            let node_id_ref = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n            if rollback.existed {\n                // Restore original value\n                if let Some(original) = &rollback.original_value {\n                    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(original).unwrap_or_default();\n                    let _ = state\n                        .client\n                        .send_to_node(\n                            topology,\n                            &node_id_ref,\n                            \"PUT\",\n                            &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                            Some(&body_bytes),\n                            &[],\n                        )\n                        .await;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // Setting didn't exist before, delete it\n                let _ = state\n                    .client\n                    .send_to_node(\n                        topology,\n                        &node_id_ref,\n                        \"DELETE\",\n                        &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                        None,\n                        &[],\n                    )\n                    .await;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn get_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"filterable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn update_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Ensure _miroir_shard is always in filterable attributes\n    let mut updated = body.0.clone();\n    if let Some(arr) = updated.as_array_mut() {\n        if !arr.iter().any(|v| v.as_str() == Some(\"_miroir_shard\")) {\n            arr.push(serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\"));\n        }\n    }\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &updated).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn delete_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Reset to default but always include _miroir_shard\n    let default = serde_json::json!([\"_miroir_shard\"]);\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &default).await\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn get_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn update_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\", &body.0).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn delete_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    delete_setting(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/sortable-attributes\nasync fn -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let Settings routes: GET, PATCH, DELETE /indexes/:index/settings\n//!\n//! Implements settings broadcast per plan §3:\n//! - Sequential apply-with-rollback on failure\n//! - Broadcast to all nodes\n//! - Rollback from successful nodes if any fail\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::write_targets,\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Settings router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(get_all_settings))\n        .route(\n            \"/filterable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_filterable_attributes).put(update_filterable_attributes).delete(delete_filterable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/searchable-attributes\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_searchable_attributes).put(update_searchable_attributes).delete(delete_searchable_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/sortable-attributes\",\n axum::routing::get(get_displayed_attributes).put(update_displayed_attributes).delete(delete_displayed_attributes),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/ranking-rules\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_ranking_rules).put(update_ranking_rules).delete(delete_ranking_rules),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/stop-words\",\n axum::routing::get(get_synonyms).put(update_synonyms).delete(delete_synonyms),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/distinct-attribute\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_distinct_attribute).put(update_distinct_attribute).delete(delete_distinct_attribute),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/typo-tolerance\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_typo_tolerance).put(update_typo_tolerance).delete(delete_typo_tolerance),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/faceting\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_faceting).put(update_faceting).delete(delete_faceting),\n        )\n        .route(\n            \"/pagination\",\n            axum::routing::get(get_pagination).put(update_pagination).delete(delete_pagination),\n        )\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings - Get all settings.\nasync fn get_all_settings(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query first available node\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n                        .unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::Null);\n                    return Ok(Json(body));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// Cached original value for rollback.\nstruct RollbackValue {\n    /// The original value to restore on rollback.\n    original_value: Option<Value>,\n    /// Whether the setting existed before.\n    existed: bool,\n}\n\n/// Generic handler for updating a setting with rollback.\nasync fn update_setting_with_rollback(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n    value: &Value,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    // Step 1: Fetch current values from all nodes for rollback\n    let mut rollback_values: std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for target in &targets {\n        let get_request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n            body: vec![],\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n        match scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], get_request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    let original_value = if r.status == 200 {\n                        Some(serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null))\n                    } else {\n                        None\n                    };\n                    rollback_values.insert(\n                        target.as_str().to_string(),\n                        RollbackValue {\n                            original_value,\n                            existed: r.status == 200,\n                        },\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Node is already down, skip rollback for it\n                rollback_values.insert(target.as_str().to_string(), RollbackValue {\n                    original_value: None,\n                    existed: false,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(value).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n    // Track successful nodes for rollback\n    let mut successful_nodes: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut last_response: Option<Value> = None;\n\n    // Sequential broadcast with rollback on failure\n    for target in &targets {\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, vec![target.clone()], request.clone(), UnavailableShardPolicy::Error)\n            .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                if let Some(r) = resp.responses.first() {\n                    if (200..300).contains(&r.status) {\n                        successful_nodes.push(target.as_str().to_string());\n                        let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&r.body).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n                        last_response = Some(body);\n                    } else {\n                        // Rollback from successful nodes\n                        rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                            \"Failed to update setting on node {}: status {}\",\n                            target.as_str(),\n                            r.status\n                        )));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Rollback from successful nodes\n                rollback_setting(state, &topology, &successful_nodes, &rollback_values, index, setting_path).await;\n                return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\n                    \"Failed to update setting on node {}: {}\",\n                    target.as_str(),\n                    e\n                )));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let response_body = if let Some(body) = last_response {\n        body\n    } else {\n        let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n            \"indexUid\": index,\n            \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n            \"type\": \"settingsUpdate\",\n            \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n        })\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(response_body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// Rollback a setting from nodes that were successfully updated.\nasync fn rollback_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n    successful_nodes: &[String],\n    rollback_values: &std::collections::HashMap<String, RollbackValue>,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) {\n    for node_id in successful_nodes {\n        if let Some(rollback) = rollback_values.get(node_id) {\n            let node_id_ref = miroir_core::topology::NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n            if rollback.existed {\n                // Restore original value\n                if let Some(original) = &rollback.original_value {\n                    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(original).unwrap_or_default();\n                    let _ = state\n                        .client\n                        .send_to_node(\n                            topology,\n                            &node_id_ref,\n                            \"PUT\",\n                            &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                            Some(&body_bytes),\n                            &[],\n                        )\n                        .await;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // Setting didn't exist before, delete it\n                let _ = state\n                    .client\n                    .send_to_node(\n                        topology,\n                        &node_id_ref,\n                        \"DELETE\",\n                        &format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                        None,\n                        &[],\n                    )\n                    .await;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn get_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"filterable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn update_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Ensure _miroir_shard is always in filterable attributes\n    let mut updated = body.0.clone();\n    if let Some(arr) = updated.as_array_mut() {\n        if !arr.iter().any(|v| v.as_str() == Some(\"_miroir_shard\")) {\n            arr.push(serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\"));\n        }\n    }\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &updated).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/filterable-attributes\nasync fn delete_filterable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    // Reset to default but always include _miroir_shard\n    let default = serde_json::json!([\"_miroir_shard\"]);\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/filterable-attributes\", &default).await\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn get_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn update_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    body: Json<Value>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    update_setting_with_rollback(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\", &body.0).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index/settings/searchable-attributes\nasync fn delete_searchable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    delete_setting(&state, &index, \"settings/searchable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/settings/sortable-attributes\nasync fn get_sortable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    get_setting(&state, &index, \"settings/sortable-attributes\").await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/:index/settings/sortable-attributes\nasync fn update_sortable_attributes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    let value: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n                        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"Invalid JSON response\"))?;\n                    return Ok(Json(value));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index))\n}\n\n/// Generic DELETE handler for resetting a setting to default.\nasync fn delete_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n        let status = axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n        return Ok((status, Json(resp.body.clone())).into_response());\n    }\n\n
